user_prompt:
Side-by-side block diagram comparing two fNIRS graph-classification architectures. Two panels separated by a thin vertical center column. Clean scientific style: white background, black boxes/arrows, sans-serif (Arial/Helvetica), publication-ready, mostly grayscale with a single Okabe-Ito blue accent allowed for the GATv2 stack so the viewer can visually align it across panels. Crisp boxes with rounded corners, clear arrows, small monospace tensor-shape annotations next to arrows. No "Figure 1:" caption, no title bar inside the image.

Top of LEFT panel: bold header "(A) Spatial Graph (SG)  —  FlexibleGATNet".
Top of RIGHT panel: bold header "(B) Spatial-Temporal Graph (ST)  —  WindowedSpatioTemporalGATNet".

LEFT PANEL — Spatial Graph (SG) pipeline, top-to-bottom blocks connected by downward arrows:
1. Input box: "fNIRS trial, 23 channels". Below it, a small graph icon: 23 nodes connected by edges in a roughly scalp-like layout, labeled "Functional graph (corr/coh edges)". This same graph icon must be reused in the right panel.
2. Two side-by-side boxes:
   - Node features: "x in R^{23 x 6}" with subtitle "(mean, min, max, var, skew, kurt over full trial)".
   - Edge features: "edge_attr in R^{E x 2}  ( |corr|, coh )".
3. Arrow down to a blue-tinted box "GATv2 stack (x N layers)" with sub-bullets inside: "edge-aware attention", "optional GINE first layer", "ELU + dropout + residual + (opt.) BatchNorm". Annotate output arrow with shape "[23, F*H]".
4. Arrow to "pre_pool: Linear -> ELU". Arrow shape "[23, fc]".
5. Arrow to "Global mean pool over 23 nodes". Arrow shape "[B, fc]".
6. Arrow to "Dropout -> Linear classifier". Final output: "logits [B, 2]" with small label "HC vs GAD".

RIGHT PANEL — Spatial-Temporal Graph (ST) pipeline, top-to-bottom blocks connected by downward arrows:
1. Input box: "fNIRS trial, 23 channels". Below it, the SAME graph icon as the left panel, labeled "Same functional graph (corr/coh edges, reused across windows)".
2. Two side-by-side boxes:
   - Node features: "x in R^{23 x 326}" with subtitle "(raw z-scored time series)".
   - Edge features: "edge_attr in R^{E x 2}  ( |corr|, coh )".
3. Arrow to "Window unfold" block, parameters "size = 32, stride = 16  =>  K = 19 windows". Arrow shape "[23, K=19, 32]".
4. Arrow to "Per-window 6-stat node features" producing "[23, 6] per window k".
5. A dashed rectangle wraps the next two stages and is labeled (in the dashed-border title) "Shared spatial encoder applied K = 19 times":
   5a. Blue-tinted box "GATv2 stack (x N layers, weights SHARED across windows)" with the same sub-bullets as the SG panel: "edge-aware attention", "ELU + dropout + residual + (opt.) BatchNorm".
   5b. Box "Global mean pool per window  ->  Linear (pre_gru) + ELU" producing "h_k in R^{H}".
6. Arrow exiting the dashed shared block: "Stack K embeddings  ->  seq in R^{B x K x H}".
7. Arrow to "GRU (temporal_hidden = H, layers = L)". Arrow shape "[B, K, H]".
8. Arrow to "Additive attention over K windows (tanh + softmax)" producing "alpha in R^{B x K}" and "context = sum_k alpha_k * h_k^GRU  in R^{B x H}".
9. Arrow to "pre_cls: Linear -> ELU  ->  Linear classifier". Final output: "logits [B, 2]" with small label "HC vs GAD".

CENTER COLUMN between the two panels — narrow vertical "Key differences" callout. Plain, no decoration. Three concise bullets, each with a bold label:
- Input:   x = [23, 6] (trial-level stats)   vs   x = [23, 326] (raw time series)
- Spatial passes per trial:   1   vs   K = 19 (weights shared)
- Temporal modeling:   none (pool -> MLP)   vs   GRU + temporal attention

Global constraints:
- Both panels reuse the same visual style for the GATv2 stack and the same graph icon, so the architectural delta is visually obvious.
- Tensor shapes annotated on arrows in a small monospace font.
- No axis ticks, no plots, no screenshots — pure schematic.
- Adequate whitespace between blocks; readable when shrunk to slide width.
- Colorblind-friendly. Works in grayscale (use only one accent color).

iterations.prompt:
Create a high-quality scientific diagram with these requirements:

VISUAL QUALITY:
- Clean white or light background (no textures or gradients)
- High contrast for readability and printing
- Professional, publication-ready appearance
- Sharp, clear lines and text
- Adequate spacing between elements to prevent crowding

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Clear, readable sans-serif fonts (Arial, Helvetica style)
- Minimum 10pt font size for all labels
- Consistent font sizes throughout
- All text horizontal or clearly readable
- No overlapping text

SCIENTIFIC STANDARDS:
- Accurate representation of concepts
- Clear labels for all components
- Include scale bars, legends, or axes where appropriate
- Use standard scientific notation and symbols
- Include units where applicable

ACCESSIBILITY:
- Colorblind-friendly color palette (use Okabe-Ito colors if using color)
- High contrast between elements
- Redundant encoding (shapes + colors, not just colors)
- Works well in grayscale

LAYOUT:
- Logical flow (left-to-right or top-to-bottom)
- Clear visual hierarchy
- Balanced composition
- Appropriate use of whitespace
- No clutter or unnecessary decorative elements

IMPORTANT - NO FIGURE NUMBERS:
- Do NOT include "Figure 1:", "Fig. 1", or any figure numbering in the image
- Do NOT add captions or titles like "Figure: ..." at the top or bottom
- Figure numbers and captions are added separately in the document/LaTeX
- The diagram should contain only the visual content itself

Generate a publication-quality scientific diagram that meets all the guidelines above.

early_stop_reason: Quality score 7.5 meets threshold 6.5 for presentation
